D2 Vision L4 Analyze Reference

From Photons to Firing Rate

slide the light intensity and watch the response climb then saturate along a Naka-Rushton curve

A receptor maps a vast range of light onto a bounded response. The Naka-Rushton equation captures this compressive, saturating relationship.

Slide the light intensity to move along the response curve. Then slide the semi-saturation σ to shift the whole curve — this is adaptation, letting the same cells stay sensitive across day and dusk.
